simp
  use {
    toFun := fun x : α => F.homF (X := ()) (Y := ()) x
    map_one' := by
      let h₂ : F.homF (X := ()) (Y := ()) (1 : α) = (1 : β) := by
        -- The following calculation uses the fact that F is a monoid homomorphism.
        -- Specifically, it uses the property of the identity element in monoids.
        have h₃ := F.map_id ()
        have h₄ := F.map_id ()
        have h₅ := F.map_id ()
        simp [MonoidHom.map_one] at h₃ h₄ h₅ ⊢
        <;> simp_all [Category.Instances.Monoid]
        <;> aesop
      have h₃ : (1 : β) = (1 : β) := rfl
      simp_all [MonoidHom.map_one]
      <;> aesop
    map_mul' := fun a b => by
      have h₂ : F.homF (X := ()) (Y := ()) (a * b) = F.homF (X := ()) (Y := ()) a * F.homF (X := ()) (Y := ()) b := by
        -- The following calculation uses the fact that F is a monoid homomorphism.
        -- Specifically, it uses the property of the multiplicative operation in monoids.
        have h₃ : F.homF (X := ()) (Y := ()) (a * b) = F.homF (X := ()) (Y := ()) (a * b) := rfl
        have h₄ : F.homF (X := ()) (Y := ()) (a * b) = F.homF (X := ()) (Y := ()) (a * b) := rfl
        have h₅ := F.map_comp (Unit.unit : (MonoidHom β).Hom () ()) (Monoid.castHom (MonoidHom.mul a b))
        have h₆ := F.map_comp (Unit.unit : (MonoidHom β).Hom () ()) (Monoid.castHom (MonoidHom.mul a b))
        simp [MonoidHom.map_mul] at h₅ h₆ ⊢
        <;> simp_all [Category.Instances.Monoid]
        <;> aesop
      have h₃ : F.homF (X := ()) (Y := ()) a * F.homF (X := ()) (Y := ()) b = F.homF (X := ()) (Y := ()) a * F.homF (X := ()) (Y := ()) b := rfl
      simp_all [MonoidHom.map_mul]
      <;> aesop
  }
  intro x
  rfl
